steps.title: Fit two points from the line
steps.content: Use the drawn line of best fit, not individual scatterplot dots. Two readable points on the line are about $(0,13.5)$ and $(10,5.5)$. Enter those in Desmos and run a two-point line calculation row.

steps.title: Read slope and intercept
steps.content: Using the two estimated points, the slope is $$m=\frac{5.5-13.5}{10-0}=-0.8$$ The point at $x=0$ gives the intercept $b=13.5$. The negative slope matches the line going down from left to right, and the positive intercept matches the line crossing the $y$-axis above $0$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The equation is $y=13.5-0.8x$. Choice A has the wrong slope sign, C has the wrong intercept sign, and D has both signs wrong. Choice B is correct.
